# stillbeat_check (Resource)

A monitored cron/heartbeat check. The job pings `ping_url` on success; a missed schedule + grace triggers alerts.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# A cron-scheduled check whose ping URL feeds straight into the job that runs it.
resource "stillbeat_check" "nightly_backup" {
  name = "nightly-backup"

  schedule = {
    kind      = "cron"
    cron_expr = "0 3 * * *"
    tz        = "Europe/Berlin"
  }

  grace    = "30m"
  channels = ["email:ops@example.com"]
}

# A simple interval check with run-too-long alerting.
resource "stillbeat_check" "hourly_sync" {
  name = "hourly-sync"

  schedule = {
    kind     = "interval"
    interval = "1h"
    tz       = "UTC"
  }

  grace        = "5m"
  max_run      = "10m"
  max_run_mode = "hung"
  channels     = ["webhook:https://example.com/hooks/stillbeat"]
}

output "backup_ping_url" {
  value = stillbeat_check.nightly_backup.ping_url
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Human-readable check name.
- `schedule` (Attributes) When the check is expected to ping. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--schedule))

### Optional

- `channels` (Set of String) Alert channels, e.g. `email:you@example.com`, `webhook:https://...`, `telegram:<chat_id>`.
- `flap_cooldown` (String) Suppress a down alert that self-resolves within this window (Go duration).
- `grace` (String) Grace period after a missed deadline before alerting, as a Go duration (e.g. `"5m"`).
- `max_run` (String) Run-too-long threshold between a `/start` and its finish (Go duration).
- `max_run_mode` (String) `hung` (sweeper flips down if unfinished) or `late` (warn on a slow finish). Defaults to `hung` when `max_run` is set.
- `nag_interval` (String) Re-alert cadence while a check stays down (Go duration). Omit to disable nagging.
- `paused` (Boolean) When true, the check is paused (not monitored). Defaults to `false`.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Server-generated check UUID.
- `ping_url` (String) The URL the job pings on success. Wire this into your cron/CI.
- `status` (String) Current status: `new`, `up`, `down`, or `paused`.

<a id="nestedatt--schedule"></a>
### Nested Schema for `schedule`

Required:

- `kind` (String) `interval` (fixed period) or `cron` (cron expression).
- `tz` (String) IANA timezone, e.g. `"UTC"` or `"Europe/Berlin"`.

Optional:

- `cron_expr` (String) Cron expression (5-field). Required when `kind = cron`.
- `interval` (String) Expected period between pings, as a Go duration (e.g. `"1h"`). Required when `kind = interval`.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# A check is imported by its ID (the UUID shown in the dashboard URL or returned
# by the API). Terraform then manages the existing check going forward.
terraform import stillbeat_check.nightly_backup 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000
```
